title: What are Python type narrowing techniques?
description: Explain how to narrow types with isinstance, TypeGuard, and TypeIs.
options: Type narrowing refines a variable's type within conditional branches. isinstance() works with type checkers automatically. TypeGuard (3.10+) narrows in if-true branch: def is_str_list(val) -> TypeGuard[list[str]]. TypeIs (3.12+) narrows in both branches (more precise). assert_type() validates at type-check time. Discriminated unions via Literal fields enable exhaustive matching
correct_answer: Type narrowing refines a variable's type within conditional branches. isinstance() works with type checkers automatically. TypeGuard (3.10+) narrows in if-true branch: def is_str_list(val) -> TypeGuard[list[str]]. TypeIs (3.12+) narrows in both branches (more precise). assert_type() validates at type-check time. Discriminated unions via Literal fields enable exhaustive matching

title: What is the difference between asyncio.TaskGroup and gather?
description: Compare asyncio.TaskGroup with asyncio.gather in Python 3.11+.
options: TaskGroup (3.11+) uses 'async with' for structured concurrency: if any task fails, all others are cancelled and errors are collected as ExceptionGroup. gather() returns when all complete; cancel_on_error requires manual handling. TaskGroup prevents orphaned tasks; gather may leave tasks running on error. TaskGroup is preferred for reliable cleanup
correct_answer: TaskGroup (3.11+) uses 'async with' for structured concurrency: if any task fails, all others are cancelled and errors are collected as ExceptionGroup. gather() returns when all complete; cancel_on_error requires manual handling. TaskGroup prevents orphaned tasks; gather may leave tasks running on error. TaskGroup is preferred for reliable cleanup

title: What are Python's buffer protocol and memoryview?
description: Explain the buffer protocol and how memoryview enables zero-copy operations.
options: The buffer protocol allows objects to expose raw memory (bytes, bytearray, array.array, numpy arrays). memoryview wraps buffer objects for zero-copy slicing and type casting without copying data. Operations on memoryview slices modify the original buffer. Supports multidimensional views, format codes (struct-like), and is critical for high-performance I/O and data processing
correct_answer: The buffer protocol allows objects to expose raw memory (bytes, bytearray, array.array, numpy arrays). memoryview wraps buffer objects for zero-copy slicing and type casting without copying data.
